site.subhead: Round 9 setup board for tipping, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 9 preview notes, charity betting discipline, and weekly NRL intel.

ticker: official round 9 team lists are now out and the board resets after the perfect 8 from 8 week
the mark hughes foundation bank stays at $6.50 after a disciplined no-bet round
bulldogs head to head is now locked for charity at $1.77 against the cowboys
knights get ponga, best and marzhew back in a major boost against the rabbitohs
broncos suddenly look dangerous again with walsh, mam, reynolds and carrigan all named
turbo is still out for manly while penrith roll in unchanged
the round 9 charity play is now locked and ready to track

featurePanels.title: Lock Pick
items: Cowboys lose Murray Taulagi while Canterbury keep their spine stable
It looks like the cleanest straight tip before late mail
The TAB number held up well enough to justify the play at $1.77 for a $10 stake
Now the job is just to track it cleanly

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Broncos get a flood of quality back and become much harder to read
Knights with Ponga and Best back are a very different side to last week
Titans versus Raiders has swing-game energy written all over it
Storm versus Dolphins can still punish anyone overreacting to recent form
Do not let the 8 from 8 round trick you into feeling invincible

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Decide whether Bulldogs are worth using for charity if the number stays playable
Track how much of a real boost the Knights get from their star returns
See if the Broncos are back to being trustworthy or just dangerous
Keep an eye on Panthers versus Manly with Turbo still sidelined
Preserve the calm process after the no-bet Round 8

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Bulldogs still look like one of the calmest sides in the comp
Knights could become a value team again if the cavalry really is back
Broncos are one of the biggest market-shift teams this week
Panthers remain ruthless even when the board starts hunting for upset angles

betCard.title: Round 9 Charity Bet
why: Canterbury still look more settled top to bottom and should control the game better.
North Queensland lose Murray Taulagi and have to reshuffle again out wide.
At $1.77 on the TAB board, the price stayed healthy enough to use for charity.
risks: The Bulldogs are without Viliame Kikau and Harry Hayes, so they are not at full strength either.
Tom Dearden and Drinkwater can still drag the Cowboys into chaos-ball contests.
The Bulldogs have still been error-prone in recent weeks, so it is not a free square.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.

watchlist: Whether the Bulldogs are still playable once the market settles.
How much the Broncos and Knights improve with key names back on deck.
Any value opening in Panthers-Manly if people over-fade Turbo's absence.
Which Round 8 reads still carry forward once the new team lists hit.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 9 opens off confidence, but not complacency
lines: As of Tuesday, 29 April 2026, official Round 9 team lists are now out and the board can move from the Round 8 wrap into fresh decision mode.
The perfect 8 from 8 week is a nice confidence boost, but it should make the process calmer rather than cockier.
The charity bank stays at $6.50 after the no-bet Round 8, so there is no pressure to force a play.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Bulldogs are now the locked charity play
lines: Canterbury still reads as the cleanest side on the board even with Kikau out, because North Queensland lose Murray Taulagi and remain less stable week to week.
At $1.77 on the TAB board, the price stayed good enough to make the play worthwhile for charity.
Broncos-Roosters and Knights-Rabbitohs both have upside, but the returning stars also make those matches more volatile.
That makes Bulldogs the calmer charity option while the swingier games stay in tipping-only territory.

sections.title: Round 9 Fixtures
sections.heading: Known Round 9 slate after Team List Tuesday

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who deserves the Round 9 upgrade or downgrade
lines: Hot: Bulldogs, Panthers, Sharks, the full-strength Knights.
Cold: Eels, the Turbo-less Sea Eagles attack, and any blind faith in last week's market lines.
The biggest swing teams are Broncos and Knights because their named line-ups are so much stronger.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points most worth checking before locking Round 9
lines: The biggest injury and availability pivots are Turbo still out, Murray Taulagi out, and the Broncos stars all returning together.
Kalyn Ponga, Bradman Best and Greg Marzhew back for Newcastle make Knights-Rabbitohs one of the trickiest reads on the slate.
Luke Metcalf on the Warriors bench and the unchanged Eels squad make Parramatta-New Zealand more competitive than the ladder suggests.
Treat the next charity bet like a fresh decision, not a carry-over from the 8 from 8 tipping high.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
Use this as a weekly operating board, then swap in live-verified NRL data when you want to publish or bet.
